articles.title: Uncle Sam is wrong, India and China are doing their bit to fight climate change
articles.summary: It is true that there is an increase in carbon emission by India and China due to homegrown consumption and lack of infrastructure. But let s keep in mind that India s carbon emissions per capita are still 10 times lower and China s is half of that compared to the US.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Taking Stock: Market fails to hold on to day s gains, ends marginally higher
articles.summary: Grasim Industries, Bharti Airtel, Nestle India, Maruti Suzuki and HCL Technologies were the biggest Nifty gainers, while losers included Sun Pharma, BPCL, Reliance Industries, MM and Hindalco Industries
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Sensex, Nifty gain for third day in a row; easing volatility to support bull trend
articles.summary: Analysts expect the market to maintain the bullish undertone as elections and corporate earnings season continue

articles.title: Agri Picks Report: Geojit
articles.summary: According to Geojit, Madhya Pradesh is preparing to spend 11.6 bln rupees under the price support scheme to procure kharif pulses--tur, urad, and moong--harvested in 2020-21 (Jul-Jun), a state government official said.

articles.summary: With hopes of another round of stimulus measures flying out of the window, the US needs more economic activity to get demand flowing.

articles.title: Rise in textile demand to support MCX cotton ahead of harvest season in October
articles.summary: Traders are also concerned about the flood-like situation that can damage the crop, which is harvested from October to February, in various parts of India.

articles.title: Agri Picks Report: Geojit
articles.summary: According to Geojit, Rubber prices rose nearly 10% over the past three-four weeks and hit a record high of 140 rupees a kg due to pent-up demand after easing of the pandemic induced lockdowns amid a supply crunch.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Oil prices slip as growing stockpiles signal bumpy fuel demand recovery
articles.summary: U.S. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude futures fell 20 cents, or 0.5%, to $37.85 a barrel at 0056 GMT, after climbing 3.5% on Wednesday.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: US EIA cuts 2020 world oil demand growth forecast
articles.summary: In its monthly forecast, the agency cut its oil demand growth estimate for 2021 by 490,000 bpd to 6.53 million bpd.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Gold may remain choppy, but buying may emerge at lower levels: Kotak Securities
articles.summary: Gold may remain choppy but buying may emerge at lower levels amid increasing global uncertainty and also as Fedâs dovish stance may limit upside in US dollar.

articles.title: Agri Picks Report: Geojit
articles.summary: According to Geojit, Global consumption of edible oils is expected to fall 30-40% in 2020 because of coronavirus lockdowns, said Dominic Xavier, Middle East and North Africa region head at Sime Darby Oils.

articles.title: Gold flat as dollar strength counters safe-haven demand
articles.summary: Spot gold was little changed at $1,929.30 per ounce by 0038 GMT, after falling to a near two-week week low of $1,906.24 on Tuesday.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Oil falls further amid growing alarm over coronavirus second wave
articles.summary: The biggest global health crisis in a century continues to flare unabated with cases rising in India, Great Britain, Spain and several parts of the United States, where the infection rate has not come under control for months.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Gold may witness choppy trade, expect buying at lower levels: Kotak Securities
articles.summary: After weeks of consolidation, crude oil has come under pressure amid signs of correction in US equity market and gains in US dollar index.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Energy Weekly Report: Motilal Oswal
articles.summary: According to Motilal Oswal, Oil prices hit a rough patch last week, falling back in line with broader financial markets. Prices did try to inch higher, but experienced selling pressure at highs.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Precious Metals Weekly Report: Motilal Oswal
articles.summary: According to Motilal Oswal, Gold fell to the lowest level in a month primarily as the dollar rose against its major crosses and better-than-expected economic number from the US.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Base Metals Weekly Report: Motilal Oswal
articles.summary: According to Motilal Oswal, Base metals traded choppy last week, with prices hitting multi month highs at the start of the week and followed by some profit taking on back of volatility in the dollar and mixed data signals from China and US.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Groww, BSE to Angel One: Over 10 capital market stocks jump up to 43% in April amid market revival
articles.summary: April marked a recovery for capital market stocks, with Groww and Angel One leading gains. The Nifty Capital Market index surged 23.20% as easing geopolitical tensions and positive earnings reports fueled investor enthusiasm, reversing four months of declines.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Private Credit’s Retail Risk Draws Scrutiny From Global Watchdog
articles.summary: The world’s top financial stability watchdog is examining the potential risks posed by an influx of retail investors into the $1.8 trillion private credit market, according to a person with knowledge of the matter.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Emerging Markets Eke Out Gains in Thin-Volume Holiday Trading
articles.summary: Emerging-market benchmarks for stocks and currencies posted small gains in holiday-thinned trade, with most markets in Asia, Europe and Africa closed for Labour Day.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Nifty 50 slips below 24,000. Next key levels to watch
articles.summary: On April 30, Indian stock indices fell nearly 1% due to rising crude oil prices and foreign fund exits. The Nifty 50 and Sensex closed at 23,997.55 and 76,913.50, respectively. Despite this, both indices showed weekly gains, breaking previous losing streaks.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Sell in May & Go Away: Is it the right strategy as US-Iran war and crude oil prices wreak havoc on portfolios?
articles.summary: Nifty 50 index has risen in six of the last 10 years during May. But the crude oil prices remain a joker in the pack for India. According to experts, the trajectory of crude oil remains the single largest short-term risk variable.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: RBL Bank, Canara Bank to PNB - Who will be impacted the most by RBI ECL norms? Explained
articles.summary: Several PSBs have indicated that the one-time provisioning hit could reduce net worth by 3–9%. In contrast, large private sector banks appear better positioned due to stronger provision buffers, estimated at 2–4% of net worth.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: GIFT City treasury hub picks up pace; 6 listed companies seek licences
articles.summary: GIFT City is gaining traction as a destination for multinational companies looking to streamline their treasury operations. Recent applications for corporate treasury centre licenses highlight a growing interest in this financial hub, offering regulatory incentives and a robust framework.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Do you know how to transfer physical shares to demat account?
articles.summary: Investors are urged to convert physical share certificates into dematerialised form due to regulatory changes. The process involves opening a demat account and submitting a Dematerialisation Request Form. This transition mitigates risks associated with physical assets and simplifies transactions.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Can HUL’s price hikes offset looming margin pressure?
articles.summary: HUL reported a multi-quarter high volume growth in Q4FY26, but rising input costs, El Niño risks and recent price hikes set up a tougher test for margins ahead.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: AMC quarterly earnings dented by impact of West Asia conflict on other income despite steady fees
articles.summary: In FY26, AMCs maintained stable earnings overall, but Q4 profits were affected by market volatility linked to the West Asia conflict. ICICI Prudential AMC and HDFC AMC saw notable declines in net profit, with mark-to-market losses impacting other income significantly.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Dr Lal Path Labs needs volume growth to clear its FY27 tests
articles.summary: Risks remain around pricing pressure in a competitive diagnostics market, continued cost pressures, and execution risks in scaling newer segments
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Stock market holiday: NSE and BSE to remain shut for 12 days in May 2026; check full list
articles.summary: The Indian stock market will be closed on Maharashtra Day and Bakri Id in May 2026, totalling 12 holidays. Trading halts include equity, derivatives, and currency segments. Key holidays like Diwali and Christmas will also see market closures, while Sensex and Nifty 50 fell amid rising oil prices.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Penny stock under ₹5 to be in focus on Monday; here’s why
articles.summary: Penny stock under ₹5: Empower India share price has rallied 12% in one week, and has jumped by a whopping 97% in one month. The penny stock has gained 50% in three months, but has risen just 2% in six months. Empower India shares have surged 30% in one year.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Indian bond yields end April near 7% as crude oil prices surge. What should investors do?
articles.summary: Global bond markets also faced pressure, with the 10-year US Treasury yield rising to a five-week high. This followed the US Federal Reserve’s most divided rate decision since 1992, which dampened expectations of near-term rate cuts as rising energy prices heightened inflation concerns.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Expert View: What's the biggest risk for HNI investors’ portfolio today? Rahul Roy Chowdhury of Geojit Financial answers
articles.summary: Expert View: When valuations were stretched, we slowed entry; now that the risk-reward balance is better, we are more willing to add selectively, said Rahul Roy Chowdhury, CEO - Private Wealth Services, Geojit Financial Services.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: OnEMI Technology Solutions IPO subscribed 24% on Day 1; GMP, review, other key details. Should you apply?
articles.summary: OnEMI Technology Solutions IPO saw a 24% subscription on its opening day, with bids for 9.4 million shares. The IPO aims to raise ₹926 crore, priced between ₹162-171 per share. It concludes on May 5, with a significant portion allocated to institutional investors.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Gold, silver prices today: Check retail rates of 24K, 22K gold, 999 silver on 1 May in Delhi, Mumbai, Pune, others
articles.summary: Gold and silver prices rose on 1 May amid geopolitical tensions and inflationary concerns, driven by rising crude oil prices. Check the latest rates for 24K, 22K gold and 999 silver in Delhi, Mumbai, Pune and other cities.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Four AC stocks to add to your watchlist as temperatures soar
articles.summary: Changing weather conditions could be setting the stage for a strong sectoral move.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rajesh Palviya of Axis Sec suggests Sun Pharma, Chennai Petro, Great Eastern Shipping shares to buy next week
articles.summary: On Thursday, Sensex and Nifty 50 declined nearly 1%, affected by increasing crude prices and foreign fund outflows. Sensex settled at 76,913.50 and Nifty 50 at 23,997.55. Markets will remain closed on Friday for Maharashtra Day.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Shares to buy or sell: Chandan Taparia of Motilal Oswal recommends three stocks to buy on Monday
articles.summary: Shares to buy or sell: Chandan Taparia has recommended three stocks to buy on Monday, 4 May 2026 - Reliance Industries, RBL Bank and BSE shares.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Crude oil prices rally on US-Iran war worries as Strait of Hormuz remains blocked; Brent above $110 per barrel
articles.summary: Crude oil prices climbed on May 1, with Brent crude at $111.59 a barrel amid unresolved tensions with Iran. Experts noted inflation fears as prices exceeded $120 for the first time in four years, following ongoing disruptions in oil supply.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Vodafone Idea share price to be in focus after ₹23,649-crore AGR relief
articles.summary: Vodafone Idea share price has gained 20% in one month, but the stock has fallen 8.5% in three months. The telecom stock has gained 17% in six months and has rallied over 43% in one year.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stocks to buy or sell for short term: Religare Broking's Ajit Mishra suggests strategies for 3 shares
articles.summary: Stocks to buy or sell for short term: Ajit Mishra, SVP of Research at Religare Broking, suggests trading strategies for three stocks, including RBL Bank and Sun Pharma. He believes a decisive move below 23,800 in Nifty could trigger further downside towards 23,500 or lower.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Rupee slide deepens in 2026: Will it hit 100 against the dollar?
articles.summary: Persistent dollar strength, foreign outflows and rising oil prices have kept the Indian rupee under pressure, even as it holds near 95—for now.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stock market holiday: BSE, NSE closed today for Maharashtra Day; Commodity trading on MCX to begin at 5 PM
articles.summary: Stock market holiday: Most major stock markets across Asia are also closed today, May 1, on account of Labour Day. Markets in China, Hong Kong, Indonesia, Singapore, Thailand, Mauritius, Malaysia, Vietnam, Taiwan, Pakistan and Sri Lanka are shut.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Gold Rebounds as Speculation of Yen Intervention Weakens Dollar
articles.summary: Gold bounced back from a three-day decline, as the dollar sank and traders weighed the prospect of a fresh US military engagement in Iran.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Treasuries Draw Buyers After Selloff as Oil Surge Stalls
articles.summary: Treasuries rose as the recent surge in oil prices stalled and bond investors were drawn to 30-year yields that topped 5% for the first time this year.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Yields ease as oil retreats from four-year high
articles.summary: USA-BONDS/ (UPDATE 2):TREASURIES-Yields ease as oil retreats from four-year high
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: AI Debt Investors Show Signs of Fatigue After $300 Billion Binge
articles.summary: After a $300 billion AI debt binge that spanned every corner of the credit market, investors are starting to show some signs of fatigue.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Hindustan Laboratories receives SEBI observations for its proposed IPO
articles.summary: Hindustan Laboratories Limited has received SEBI observations for its proposed IPO of up to 1,41,00,000 equity shares. The funds will support working capital and corporate needs. The company primarily manufactures generic medicines for government institutions.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Apple shares gain as investors await second quarter earnings
articles.summary: Wall Street analysts anticipate earnings per share of $1.95 on revenue of $109.7 billion. This would represent a 15% year-over-year increase from the $95.4 billion reported in the same period last year
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Meta shares slump 10%, hit two-week low as AI spending concerns overshadow Q1 beat
articles.summary: Meta shares dropped 10.3% to $600, its largest decline in six months, amid fears that AI investments may not yield quick returns. The company reported Q1 sales of $56.3 billion but faced concerns about rising costs and declining user engagement.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Pulse of the Street: Will the April sprint stumble over $126 oil and a 95-plus Rupee?
articles.summary: Geopolitical tensions, elevated crude and a falling rupee are intensifying strains on sentiment, even as broader markets saw record participation in April. Financials led the decline this week amid regulatory shocks and profit booking.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: OnEMI Technology IPO: Issue subscribed 0.25 times on Day 01; GMP signals flat listing
articles.summary: OnEMI Technology's IPO for its digital lending platform Kissht opened on April 29, receiving only 0.25 times subscription on the first day. The issue includes a fresh issue of 4.97 crore shares and an OFS of 0.44 crore shares, totaling ₹925.92 crore.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Multibagger stock hits record high, logs 10% intraday gain after Q4 results 2026
articles.summary: Mangalam Worldwide shares rose 9.33% to ₹351, driven by strong Q4 FY26 results. Net profit surged to ₹15.38 crore, while revenue from operations was ₹264.95 crore. The company declared a final dividend of ₹0.30 per share and plans a direct listing on BSE.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Centre, states collect record ₹2.43 trillion in gross April GST revenue
articles.summary: Last April, the Centre and states had collected ₹2.37 trillion in GST revenue before adjusting for refunds, but the figures are not strictly comparable because of the GST cuts in September 2025.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Government lowers export duty on diesel, ATF
articles.summary: Windfall levies were first imposed in 2022 during the peak of the Russia-Ukraine war and were withdrawn in 2024.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India plans ₹51,000 crore push to procure 62 ships amid West Asia crisis
articles.summary: The government wants to significantly scale up India’s shipping capacity, including container vessels, LPG carriers, crude oil tankers, and environment-friendly green tugs, as geopolitical tensions disrupt maritime trade routes.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: FDI inflows set to top $90 billion in FY26, says DPIIT Secretary Bhatia
articles.summary: FDI inflows are set to exceed $90 billion in FY26, with April–February figures already at $88 billion, driven by reforms, free trade agreement progress and strong growth.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Eight states peg fiscal gap at 3%; UP revenue-surplus, Punjab most indebted
articles.summary: Eight Indian states, including Uttar Pradesh and Bihar, have set their fiscal deficit at 3% or below GSDP, indicating improved budget discipline. States like Punjab and Himachal Pradesh struggle with high liabilities.
articles.label: NEUTRAL
